notes: Recognisably the same squid in the same orientation but distorted, with both long tentacles dropped and a noisy fragmented outline that breaks the FAO single-weight ink feel.
specific_issues: both long tentacles from the photo (the one arching up over the mantle and the one trailing below) are entirely missing
arms reduced to short stubs with no ring suckers
mantle/fin proportions distorted — fin reads as a separate triangular wing rather than the continuous lateral fin in the photo
outline is jagged and broken instead of a clean continuous silhouette
stipple is sparse and scattered, not following the dense chromatophore field visible on the mantle in the photo
edge noise and floating speckles read as photo-trace artifacts rather than scientific plate restraint
eye marked but head region collapsed so arm crown geometry is unclear
